description: Narrative findings from the xebench cross-engine benchmark, published as data so consumers render the lab's wording verbatim and never re-word a claim. Schema: data/findings.schema.json.

findings.headline: Missing from this board: LiteRT-LM on NPU â€” and exactly why
findings.deck: NPU is LiteRT-LM's flagship capability, yet no third party can currently reproduce it â€” the runtime ships, the bridge libs don't.
claims: NPU acceleration is LiteRT-LM's flagship capability â€” Google reports up to ~12Ă— CPU / 10Ă— GPU for LLMs on a Qualcomm/MediaTek NPU.
It is absent here because no third party can currently reproduce it.

We built the full stack on a Galaxy S23 (Snapdragon 8 Gen 2): Qualcomm's QAIRT 2.48.40 runtime loads, the Hexagon v73 DSP skel is present, and the model parses through to its decode-signature quantization â€” then it stops.

The missing pieces are the LiteRTâ†”Qualcomm bridge libraries (libLiteRtDispatch_Qualcomm.so, libGemmaModelConstraintProvider.so), which Google builds from internal sources and does not publish as prebuilt binaries.
The Qualcomm hardware libraries are available; the Google glue that connects them is not.

steps.label: LiteRTâ†”Qualcomm bridge libs
steps.detail: libLiteRtDispatch_Qualcomm.so, libGemmaModelConstraintProvider.so â€” Google does not publish prebuilt

findings.resolution: Publishing those libs is all it would take for a real LiteRT-NPU row to appear here.
